summary: A heavily stylized DHS montage built around the slogan/title card 'REMIGRATE,' mixing a deliberate VHS/glitch grain filter over aerial surveillance footage with dramatic serif title cards and edited clips of immigration arrests and detention processing. The aesthetic (retro-degraded filter, stark black title card, fast cuts of cuffed detainees) is engineered as shareable ideological spectacle rather than informational reporting. Paired with grandiose civilizational rhetoric ('inheritors of the most incredible civilization... Our job is to defend it'), it markets mass deportation as a heroic, edgy, online-native cause, fitting the youth-appeal produced category.

script.scene: Aerial/overhead shot of a lone figure standing on pavement, rendered through a heavy black-and-white VHS static/glitch grain filter for a degraded, ominous aesthetic.
Stark full-screen black title card with bold white serif text 'REMIGRATE' centered.
Sun-lit street scene by a Mobil gas station: a man in a backwards cap and tan shirt being detained/escorted by a pl-clothes agent (face blurred); patrol vehicle tail-light in foreground.
Motion-blurred whip-pan transition over grass and a roadway, used as a kinetic cut between scenes.
Interior of a processing facility: two men in handcuffs, one in a blue patterned shirt and one in a dark sweater, stand against a cinderblock wall being processed/moved through a doorway.

notable_text: On-screen title card: 'REMIGRATE'. Tweet: 'Americans are the inheritors of the most incredible civilization that has ever existed. Our job is to defend it.'
